title: Deliciously Spicy Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A fresh and cozy Tunisian dish featuring lamb, tabasco sauce, and a blend of spices, perfect for a cold winter night, this recipe is also raw-friendly and cure-based.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the tabasco sauce, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the chopped dried apricots and stir to combine.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened, about 1 1/2 hours.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
